title: sempervivum tectorum subsp. calcareum
intro: Sempervivum tectorum subsp. calcareum, also known as the houseleek or chalk houseleek, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the genus Sempervivum. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Sempervivum tectorum subsp. calcareum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and boosting the immune system. Traditionally, this plant is prepared as a tea, salve, or tincture, and it can also be applied topically to the affected area to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
